title: glaucium refractocarpum subsp. erectocarpum
intro: Glaucium refractocarpum subsp. erectocarpum, commonly known as the Turkish poppy or Syrian poppy, is a plant species belonging to the Papaveraceae family, characterized by its erect stem and blue to purple flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, derived from its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Glaucium refractocarpum subsp. erectocarpum include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as potential therapeutic applications for conditions such as epilepsy and anxiety. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
